We recommended that Management require the ineligible beneficiaries to refund the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱1,686,000.00 are collected and returned to DSWD. We enjoined the responsible officials to initiate the collection efforts for the refund by sending demand letters.

We recommended that BAC submit a valid explanation in regards to the deficiencies observed. Prospectively, BAC should ensure that the Municipality abides with the standards set forth in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended further that BAC be enhanced/ updated with the capacity and skills to plan/implement procurement procedures in accordance with the law by attending necessary trainings on IRR of RA 9184 and visiting the GPPB website for new issuances and advisories issued.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit valid justification on the conduct of procurements without adherence to the mandatory periods allowed for each procurement activity as required in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of Item 3.5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and the required OSS form. We also enjoined BAC to be continually updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to submit the As-Built Plans for the subject completed infrastructure projects. Henceforth, As-Built Plans shall be required by the Municipal Accountant in the processing of final payments for all completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the BAC comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as required in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.   Henceforth, we enjoined the BAC to be continually updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid issues of non-compliance in the future.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that a MOA requiring liquidation report be executed between the LGU and implementing agency prior to fund release. Also the Municipal Accountant should certify that funds previously released to the requesting agency had been liquidated, verified and accounted for in the books. Further, that all disbursements for financial assistance be supported with complete documentation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 | Financial assistance in 2015 was not supported with a MOA because the management believed that the Sangguniang Bayan resolution was enough to support the assistance. After 2015, releases were already supported with MOA between DepEd and the LGU.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant had implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It was recommended that the Accounting and Treasury units should strictly enforce the provisions of GAA 2010 pertaining to the statutory net pay, to support in the campaign for an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel will receive adequate salary for their daily subsistence.  Also, all salary payments to persons other than the payee should be with written authority from the payee to avoid future misunderstanding and/or arguments.
observations.managementAction: CY 2010 | Employees’ loans were minimized to comply with the net pay requirement.
observations.reason: Substantially complied with the net pay requirement

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to
submit proof of posting of all stages of their procurement activities in regards to this project
in the PhilGEPS website to avoid suspension or disallowance of this transaction.

We recommend that Management expedite the implementation of the said
projects funded under BUB, following its existing guidelines to avoid the penalties
attached on non-implementation.

We also recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement,
duly executed by and between the Municipality of Catmon and DILG.

We recommend that the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014 be filled up
immediately by the Municipality, instead of designating existing employees to
comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC)
No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We reiterate our recommendation that Management should comply with the
provisions of Section 9 of Republic Act No. 5447 on the timely remittance of the
Special Education Fund.

We recommend that Management comply with the provisions in the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA 9184 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit, and to comply
with all requirements in the acquisition of motor vehicle as provided in the DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014 prior to the
procurement process.

We recommend that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to
submit printed copies of notices posted in PhilGEPS to avoid suspension and/or
disallowance of the transactions.

We recommend that the procuring entity stop the practice of splitting purchase
requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular
No. 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement
process to obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the
government, and to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should ensure that the programs and
activities of the 20% Development Fund be implemented and optimized for the
benefit of the constituents of the Municipality.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Agency Head require the Accountant,
Budget Officer and Treasurer to comply with the regulations on the timely
submission of financial reports particularly COA Circular 2010-001, Section 6.05
of COA Circular 95-006 and Section 70 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I.
Otherwise, institute appropriate administrative sanctions pursuant to Section 122
(2) of PD 1445.

We recommend for the immediate deposit of collections by the Municipal
Treasurer in conformity with COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 to safeguard
government funds from risk of loss.

We again reiterate our recommendation that the Local School Board limit the utilization of the Special Education Fund to the programs and projects defined under Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447 and the priority listing provided in Section 100 and Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code, and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG, in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could avail under this fund.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the stated recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Person be directed to submit the certification of the DILG approving the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that the activities contained therein are in consonance with the criteria stipulated in RA 9710 addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction:
Management said that they had submitted the amount of ₱3,166,491.00 of the municipality has no approval from DILG, Cebu Province for review, however this was returned with comments and suggestions. The revised plan was not resubmitted, thus there was no certification from DILG.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Person be directed to submit the certification of the DILG approving the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that the activities contained therein are in consonance with the criteria stipulated in RA 9710 addressing gender issues.

Management will comply
